title: cornus sanguinea subsp. australis
intro: Cornus sanguinea subsp. australis, also known as blood dogwood, is a plant species that belongs to the Cornaceae family, characterized by its vibrant red foliage and small white flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Cornus sanguinea subsp. australis have been found to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal effects.
